name: Shelled Gravite
desc: [NOT IMPL Not Functional]

name: Ferric Stone
desc: Although it's not completely pure, this ore is only exploited for its iron, and contains no other ore that could be efficiently harvested.

name: Ferrous Outcrop
desc: Commonly found within the same clusters as ferric stones, these impure outcrops combine smaller iron nuggets with other crystals like quartz or hematite.

name: Magnetite
desc: Named after its principal component, this ore is also rich in other crystals including quartz, graphite and even diamond.

name: Cupric Stone
desc: Although it's not completely pure, this ore is only exploited for its copper, and contains no other ore that could be efficiently harvested.

name: Brassy Outcrop
desc: Commonly found within the same clusters as cupric stones, these impure outcrops combine smaller copper nuggets with other crystals like quartz or azurite.

name: Chalcocite
desc: An especially profitable copper ore, chalcocite is rich in copper, but can also contain a large variety of other crystals.

name: Tenorite
desc: A rare and hard ore of copper, tenorite is sometimes called black copper. It often contains precious inclusions.

name: Azurite
desc: Azurite crystal is too valuable in itself to be broken down into separate elements, even though it contains large quantities of copper and carbon.

name: Malachite
desc: Malachite crystal is too valuable in itself to be broken down into separate elements, even though it contains large quantities of copper and carbon.

name: Zircon
desc: Named after its principal component, zircon was formerly traded as a gemstone, but has become essential for industrial uses, and nuclear reactors in particular.

name: Coal Clump
desc: This ore contains several forms of carbonite crystals, ranging from coal and graphite to small diamonds.

name: Vitreous Carbon
desc: This carbon ore contains a surprisingly high amount of graphite.

name: Graphite
desc: This pure graphite ore is the most stable form of carbon.

name: Gigantic Diamonds
desc: Had these crystals been discovered in the Milky Way era, they would have severely disrupted the global economy.

name: Sandstone Mound
desc: Although this sedimentary rock can be used in its current state, it also contains large quantities of quartz, aquamarine and iron, among other minerals.

name: Gray Quartz
desc: The coloration of quartz is determined by its impurities. Rare impurities can actually make it more valuable, but this one just has ordinary impurities.

name: White Quartz
desc: The coloration of quartz is determined by its impurities. Rare impurities can actually make it more valuable, but this one has none.

name: Red Quartz
desc: The coloration of quartz is determined by its impurities. This one has exactly the kind of impurities that can make quartz more valuable.

name: White Feldspar Mound
desc: This ore, mostly made up of aluminum and sandstone, can contain crystals ranging from aquamarine to emerald.

name: Aluminum Reduction
desc: Under the influence of deep submarine current, aluminium can be found in native form.

name: Turquoise Stone
desc: Turquoise is too valuable in itself to be broken down into separate elements, even though it contains large quantities of copper and aluminum.

name: Turquoise Clump
desc: Commonly found within the same clusters as turquoise stones, these impure clumps can contain varying proportions of aquamarine and emerald.

name: Aquamarine
desc: Although it's not completely pure, this ore only contains crystals that are at least as valuable as pure aquamarine.

name: Emerald
desc: This beryl variety is too valuable in itself to be broken down into separate elements, even though it contains large quantities of aluminum and silicon.

name: Vanadinite
desc: This common vanadium chlorure is easy to recognize with its characteristic shape and color.

name: Patronite
desc: This ore, mostly made up of vanadium and sulfur, can contain crystals ranging from quartz to diamond.

name: Titanite
desc: Typically found on asteroids, this ore is an aggregate of small titanium nuggets.

name: Plagnetite
desc: Generally found in asteroids, this formation contains an equal amount of iron and platinium.

name: Cooperite
desc: One of the most common planetary platinium ore, cooperite is also a source of a much more mundane element, sulfur.

name: Uraninite Outcrop
desc: This crystalline ore is the purest natural occurrence of uranium.

name: Elmerite
desc: Essential to contemporary industry, elmerium can only be found in these strange clusters that appear on the dark side of celestial objects.

name: Hollow Outcrop
desc: Commonly found within the same clusters as elmerite, these impure outcrops combine smaller elmerium fragments with chunks of sandstone.

name: Siderite
desc: Named after iron, its principal component, this ore is also rich in carbon and can contain quartz, graphite and hematite.

name: Chalcopyrite
desc: Formerly known as "yellow copper", this ore is much more diverse than the name suggests, containing substantial quantities of pyrite, graphite, quartz and iron.

name: Sulphuric Stone
desc: Named after its principal component, this ore is also rich in other crystals, including pyrite and vanadium.

name: Titanomagnetite
desc: Essentially made up of iron and titanium, this ore is also rich in hematite, quartz and graphite.

name: Augurite
desc: This volcanic rock is often rich in aluminium and titanium.

name: Bauxite Rock
desc: This sedimentary stone is incredibly rich in diverse crystals, ranging from calcite to emerald.

name: Wolframite
desc: Wolframite get its name from wolfram, an ancient name of tungsten, its more remarkable component.

name: Cinnabar
desc: This mineral is a crystal of mercury sulfide. It was widely used as pigment because of its bright red color, before the high toxicity or mercury was recognized.

name: Pyrite Formation
desc: Pyrite is a simple and common iron sulfide. Because of its yellow shine, it has often been mistaken for gold.

name: Tungsten Reduction
desc: Under the influence of deep submarine current, tungsten can be found in native form.

name: Iron Deposit
desc: This pure iron vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Dense Iron Deposit
desc: This pure iron vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory. Its unusual density allows for a higher extraction rate.

name: Coal Deposit
desc: This pure coal vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Sulfur Deposit
desc: This pure sulfur vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Sandstone Deposit
desc: This pure sandstone vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Copper Deposit
desc: This pure copper vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Dense Copper Deposit
desc: This pure copper vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory. Its unusual density allows for a higher extraction rate.

name: Titanium Deposit
desc: This pure titanium vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Vanadium Deposit
desc: This pure vanadium vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Pyrite Deposit
desc: This pure pyrite vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Aluminum Deposit
desc: This pure aluminum vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Dense Aluminum Deposit
desc: This pure aluminum vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory. Its unusual density allows for a higher extraction rate.

name: Platinum Deposit
desc: This pure platinum vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Dense Platinum Deposit
desc: This pure platinum vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory. Its unusual density allows for a higher extraction rate.

name: Tungsten Deposit
desc: This pure tungsten vein contains enough ore to supply an entire factory.

name: Brine Pool
desc: This pool, formed by centuries of precipitation, provides a seemingly infinite source of water.

name: Vitriol Pool
desc: This pool, formed by centuries of precipitation, provides a seemingly infinite source of vitriol.

name: Mercury Pool
desc: [DEPRECATED This pool, formed by centuries of precipitation, provides a seemingly infinite source of mercury.

name: Basalt Shell
desc: Beneath this thick shell of volcanic rock lies an unusual concentration of rare minerals.

name: Clay Shell
desc: Beneath this thick sedimentary shell lies an unusual concentration of kaolinite.

name: Chalcedony Shell
desc: Inside this thick, hardened geode lies an unusual concentration of crystals.

name: Iron Spherule
desc: Inside this thick, hardened geode lies an unusual concentration of hematite. This crystal formation can only appear on asteroids.

name: Ice Shell
desc: [NOT IMPL(only in cold or even very cold planet)

name: Volcanic Shell
desc: [NOT IMPL (only in hot or even very hot planet)

name: Pumice
desc: [NOT IMPL (relatively frail volcanic pillow)

name: Cinder
desc: [NOT IMPL (a little less frail volcanic pillow)

name: Chondrite
desc: [NOT IMPL (Meteoritic Shell)

name: Water Geyser
desc: Though intermittent, the water supply this geyser provides is both reliable and renewable.

name: Brine Water Geyser
desc: Though intermittent, the water supply this geyser provides is both reliable and renewable.

name: Vitriol Geyser
desc: Though intermittent, the vitriol supply this geyser provides is both reliable and renewable.

name: Mercury Geyser
desc: Though intermittent, the mercury supply this geyser provides is both reliable and renewable.

name: Radon Geyser
desc: Though intermittent, the radon supply this geyser provides is both reliable and renewable.
